docs.title: Urbano Versions
docs.text:
The Grasshopper plugin currently exists in two versions, please see below.

  • Urbano v1

    Our initial release, currently not maintained.

    View v1 Archive

  • Urbano v2

    Our current release, actively maintained.

    Get Started with v2

docs.title: Urbano v1
docs.text: Please find the component documentation on the following pages. You can use the sidebar navigation to browse all available components, or press / to quickly search for what you need.

docs.title: 🚀 Features
docs.text:
A preview of what Urbano enables you to do

  • DOWNLOAD GEOSPATIAL DATA

    Download maps and points-of-interest data from OpenStreetMap directly in Grasshopper.

  • IMPORT & AGGREGATE DATA

    Parse and merge layers from OSM or shapefiles into a single coherent project model.

  • LOOK-UP & MODIFY METADATA

    Attach, query and parametrically edit metadata on any geometric object.

  • BUILD MOBILITY MODEL

    Auto-generate a topological street network and building access points ready for simulation.

  • MULTI-MODAL ROUTING

    Compute fastest paths for pedestrians, cyclists or cars between origins and destinations.

  • SIMULATE WITH TRIP ENGINE

    Launch activity-based simulations using Amenity Demand Profiles (ADP).

  • ANALYSE AMENITIES & STREETS

    Evaluate Streetscore, Amenityscore and Walkscore to assess vitality and accessibility.

  • INTEGRATED CAD WORKFLOW

    Bake geometry with metadata back to Rhino and visualise results instantly.

docs.title: 📊 Metrics
docs.text:
• AMENITY DEMAND PROFILE

    Spatiotemporal distribution of human activities; default data provided and fully editable.

  • STREETSCORE

    Counts Street Hits to show how many simulated trips use each street segment.

  • AMENITYSCORE

    Compares amenity demand (Amenity Hits) with supply to reveal over- or under-served areas.

  • WALKSCORE

    Calculates a 0-100 walkability rating with customisable weightings.

docs.title: 🛠️ Samples
docs.text:
Use cases with Urbano components

  • URBANO TEMPLATES

    Import Urbano Template - starter files that demonstrate each core workflow.

  • ROUTING

    Router - compute shortest paths, distances and travel times for multiple traffic modes.

  • ACCESSIBILITY WITHIN DISTANCE

    Router - visualise all buildings reachable from an origin within a chosen threshold.

  • TRIPS FROM GIVEN ORIGIN

    ADP Trip Engine & Inspect Trip - split population by ADP and send trips to valid amenities.

  • STREETSCORE (ADD LINK)

    ADP Trip Engine & Street Hits - add or remove links and see Street Hits update instantly.

  • AMENITYSCORE (ADD POPULATION)

    ADP Trip Engine & Amenity Hits - add building occupants and observe nearby amenity utilisation.

  • AMENITYSCORE (ADD AMENITIES)

    ADP Trip Engine & Amenity Hits - insert new amenities to balance supply and demand.

  • AMENITYSCORE (ADP TIME STEPS)

    ADP & Trip Engine - run 24-hour simulations to capture temporal variations.

  • WALKSCORE (ADD AMENITIES)

    Trip Engine & Walkscore - add missing amenities to improve walkability scores.

docs.title: ADP Trip Engine
docs.text: Generate trips from the building based on the given activity demand profile and the destination choice model for each activity in the profile

docs.title: Model []
docs.text: Urbano model containing buildings with amenities and network

docs.title: ADPer []
docs.text: Activity Demand Profile (trips are produced for each activity entry in the profile

docs.title: DestChoice []
docs.text: Destination choice model for each activity. It defines whether to produce one or multiple trips per activity in the ADP

docs.title: FacKey []
docs.text: A necessary input for By-Destination-Factor model. Please ensure the input/selected metadata keys are existing in the model.

docs.title: TimeLmt []
docs.text: A necessary input for By-Destination-Factor model. It defines the travel time limit when searching for the available destinations.

docs.title: Build []
docs.text: Toggle to start building Urbano model

docs.title: Trips
docs.text: Trips generated by the engine according to the ADP

docs.title: Activity Demand Profile / Persona
docs.text: Activity Demand Profile describing the population weighted demand of activities for a certain persona type. Either input profile data, or load from stock data provided by Urbano. Loaded data can then be edited and saved in the ADP editor. You can even start creating a profile from scratch in the editor

docs.title: ActDem []
docs.text: Activities and their corresponding weights in comma separated lists

docs.title: Load []
docs.text: Load Activity Demand Profile data in CSV format from the dropdown / File Path / URL

docs.title: Index []
docs.text: One-based index for the column to use if the ADP data includes multiple columns (normally to indicate different time intervals)

docs.title: Norm []
docs.text: Normalize the demand weights to sum to 1

docs.title: ADPer
docs.text: Activity Demand Profile describing the weighted demand of activities of a certain persona type

docs.title: Amenity Capacity
docs.text: Calculate amenity capacity based on the area and activity

docs.title: Area []
docs.text: Built area of the amenity, assign -1 to use default area for the activity

docs.title: LogProp []
docs.text: Logistics area proportion of the amenity, default = 0.2

docs.title: Meta
docs.text: Metadata containing the "ameinty_capacity" value (deconstruct to use the value itself)

docs.text: Hits at amenities in the given trips

docs.title: Trips []
docs.text: Trips that are used to compute hits

docs.title: Amen
docs.text: Amenity hosting certain activities for people to do

docs.title: AmHits
docs.text: A counter that tallies up the total number of people that are sent to a certain amenity on all trips

docs.title: AmTrips

Providing the file path input will copy the data to the given location and can then be used to import later

docs.title: Trip Amenityscore
docs.text: Amenityscore computed for the amenities based on the given trips. 'Amenityscore = 0' indicates the balance between the footfall and the capacity (Amenityscore = Amenity Hits / Amenity Capacity - 1).

docs.title: Urbano v2
docs.text:
Welcome to the Urbano v2 component documentation!

You can use the sidebar navigation to browse all available components, or press / to quickly search for what you need.

Your browser does not support embedded videos. You can download the teaser video here.

docs.title: Osm Feature Query
docs.text: Generate OSM feature queries suitable for input to the Import Osm File

docs.title: Osm Feature Query [Text list]
docs.text: Define one or more OpenStreetMap tag queries to filter features.              Query syntax:             • x=y → Exact key–value match             Example: amenity=parking             • x=* → Any value for a given key             Example: building=*             • *=y → Any key with a given value             Example: *=school             • (x) → Vague text search (key or value)             Example: (parking)              Input format:             Provide a list of query strings. Each query is evaluated independently and combined as a union.

docs.title: Osm Features [Text list]
docs.text: Define one or more OpenStreetMap tag queries to filter features.              Query syntax:             • x=y → Exact key–value match             Example: amenity=parking             • x=* → Any value for a given key             Example: building=*             • *=y → Any key with a given value             Example: *=school             • (x) → Vague text search (key or value)             Example: (parking)              Input format:             Provide a list of query strings. Each query is evaluated independently and combined as a union.

docs.title: Urbano Gradient
docs.text: Represents a multiple colour gradient. The Reverse and Transparency inputs modify the gradient permanently.

docs.title: Reverse [Boolean]
docs.text: Reverse the gradient. Use a button for one-shot action.

docs.title: Walkscore Component
docs.text: Compute Walkscore values for input geometries using Itinero routing to amenity POIs extracted from OSM.

docs.title: Geometries [Geometry list]
docs.text: Geometries to evaluate (mesh, curve, point, etc.). Center points are used as walkscore locations.

docs.title: Run [Boolean]
docs.text: Set to true to compute walkscores. When false, outputs previously cached results.

docs.title: Dir [Text list]
docs.text: Additional folders to import templates from
